Keep Unicode subjects visible in previews 🚀

Commit summaries now preserve names such as Zoë and 東京 when the editor
reloads a draft. Keep the body concise and retain intentional blank lines.

Signed-off-by: Avery Chen <avery@example.test>

# Please enter the commit message for your changes. Lines starting
# with '#' will be ignored, and an empty message aborts the commit.
#
# On branch feature/unicode-preview
# Your branch is up to date with 'origin/feature/unicode-preview'.
#
# Changes to be committed:
#	modified:   src/preview.rs
#	new file:   tests/unicode_preview.rs
#	deleted:    tests/legacy_ascii.rs
#	renamed:    docs/preview.txt -> docs/preview.md
# Untracked files:
#	note:       scratch/review-notes.txt
diff --git a/src/preview.rs b/src/preview.rs
index 5d7e3a1..9c421ef 100644
--- a/src/preview.rs
+++ b/src/preview.rs
@@ -18,4 +18,5 @@ pub fn subject(draft: &Draft) -> String {
     let first = draft.lines().next().unwrap_or_default();
-    first.trim().to_owned()
+    let normalized = first.trim_end_matches(['\r', '\n']);
+    normalized.to_owned()
 }
